channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: League of Legends Next Might Let You Queue in Three Roles
item.content:encoded:
Dataminers have allegedly discovered a major change that is potentially coming to League of Legends next year.

The League Next project is rebuilding LoL from the ground up, with a new client and large systemic changes.

However, details on the major update are sparse, with an announcement expected sometime between the MSI 2026 and LoL Worlds 2026 esports tournaments.

Now, it appears that Riot is experimenting with allowing players to choose three roles to queue up for.

LoL dataminer LoLLeaksEN revealed code that suggests Riot will eventually allow a third position, “tertiary,” to be picked during role selection.

What Does This Change Mean for LoL players?

Giving players the choice of three roles should significantly speed up wait times for getting into matches, particularly at higher ranks.

League Next fixing LoL’s broken client and making major improvements to the game’s backend would certainly make this more complex queueing system possible.

However, it could also highly affect the quality of ranked matches, with many players typically struggling on their secondary roles, let alone their third-choice.

Though it could be argued that it would prevent more instances of players being autofilled into a role that they didn’t choose if queue times are too high.

Riot tried to address issues with autofilling for the 2026 ranked season, making it so that the system would first try to match an autofilled player against another autofilled player in the same role.

Riot also introduced the Aegis of Valor system, which rewards players with either double LP or full protection from LP loss on an autofilled game if they receive a mastery score of C or higher.

These changes were potentially brought in to make the release of this third role option smoother for League Next.

League Next will also overhaul the support role according to Riot Games’ senior vice president Andrei ‘Meddler’ van Roon.

What do you make of the possible expansion of role selection to three positions? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

item.title: Esports Nation Cup: EA FC 26 Qualification Explained
item.content:encoded:
How EA Sports FC 26 players can qualify for the Esports Nations Cup and the regional distribution for qualification spots has been confirmed by the tournament’s website.

While not taking place until the middle of November, the ENC event will still be played on EA FC 26 despite the fact that the next iteration in the game series will likely have been released by then.

While hundreds of esports coaches have been confirmed for the ENC, Insider Gaming has been informed that the EA FC 26 tournament will strangely not allow coaches.

The upcoming EA FC event will see 128 players compete for national pride and a share of the $600k prize pool.

However, only a maximum of two players can represent a country at the tournament, which will lead to some fierce competition.

For example, in the current EA FC Pro World Rankings, there are three Dutch players in the top 10: Emre Yilmaz, Manuel Bachoore, and Levi de Weerd.

The top 64 players in the FC Pro World Rankings will receive a direct invite to the event. However, only one player from each nation can receive a direct invite.

Another 56 spots will then be filled via online regional qualifiers, with limited spots distributed to each region.

Then, the final eight spots will be awarded to ‘Wildcards,’ though no further details have been confirmed so far. This will likely mean there will be a Last Chance Qualifier just before the event.

EA FC 26 — ENC Regional Distribution

The limited number of qualifier slots for each regional event will be distributed as follows:

  • Europe East — 14 national players
  • Europe West — 8 national players
  • LATAM South — 6 national players
  • Middle East — 4 national players
  • Africa — 6 national players
  • Asia South — 4 national players
  • Asia North — 4 national players
  • LATAM North — 6 national players
  • North America — 2 national players
  • Oceania — 2 national players

EA FC 26 will be one of the 16 games at the inaugural Esports Nations Cup.

Which nation do you expect to win the EA FC ENC tournament? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Escape from Tarkov’s First Season Will Arrive in July
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has confirmed that Escape from Tarkov will get its first core season in July. This will add a brand-new character category for everyone to explore, alongside the PvP and PvE offerings. In an update on today’s TarkovTV broadcast, it was outlined exactly how seasons will work, with the studio’s Nikita Buyanov stating there are thousands of years of seasons coming.

That’s a joke, but he did stress that one season will naturally follow another. The team shared a couple of screenshots to explain how things will work, including the difficulty modifiers that users will be able to apply from the start of every new season.

Tarkov’s Seasons Draw Closer

In July, the first Escape from Tarkov season, dubbed Kord Breach, will arrive. It’s centered around the Black Division faction, the mysterious unit that was revealed but not expanded on when Tarkov hit 1.0 in November.

This is the first time players will be able to use a seasonal character, which stands alongside their regular characters:

From what we can see in the image, seasons are planned to last at least 74 days.

When a new season arrives, players will be able to pick from a range of positive and negative modifiers, which can alter their experience and give them buffs or nerfs based on how much of a challenge (or easy ride) the player wants.

This was compared to some kind of RPG system by the team.

Also, the first season, Kord Breach, will introduce new clothing lines, themed around the Black Division outfits, which many players have been asking for since they were revealed last year.

Do you think Tarkov’s seasonal system will be a win? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Escape from Tarkov’s ICEBREAKER event is coming in May

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
